questions.question:
When an interrupt occurs, an operating system

(GATE CSE 1997)
options:
B.

always changes state of interrupted process after processing the interrupt

C.

always resumes execution of interrupted process after processing the interrupt

D.

may change state of interrupted process to 'blocked' and schedule another process.
questions.correct_answer:
D.

may change state of interrupted process to 'blocked' and schedule another process.
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
I/O redirection

(GATE CSE 1997)
options:
A.

implies changing the name of a file

B.

can be employed to use an existing file as input file for a program
questions.correct_answer:
B.

can be employed to use an existing file as input file for a program

questions.question:
The correct matching for the following pairs is:
\(\small \begin{array}{cl|cl}\hline \text{(A)} &\text{Disk Scheduling} & \text{(1)} &\text{Round robin} \\\hline \text{(B)} &\text{Batch Processing} & \text{(2)} &\text{SCAN} \\\hline \text{(C)} & \text{Time-sharing} & \text{(3)}&\text {LIFO} \\\hline \text{(D)} &\text{Interrupt processing} & \text{(4)} &\text{FIFO} \\\hline \end{array}\)

(GATE CSE 1997)

questions.question:
Locality of reference implies that the page reference being made by a process

(GATE CSE 1997)
options:
A.

will always be to the page used in the previous page reference

B.

is likely to be to one of the pages used in the last few page references

C.

will always be to one of the pages existing in memory

D.

will always lead to a page fault
questions.correct_answer:
B.

is likely to be to one of the pages used in the last few page references
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Four jobs to be executed on a single processor system arrive at time 0 in the order A, B, C, D. Their burst CPU time requirements are 4, 1, 8, 1 time units respectively. The completion time of A under round robin scheduling with time slice of one time unit is

(GATE CSE 1996)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
A solution to the Dining Philosophers Problem which avoids deadlock is to

(GATE CSE 1996)
options:
A.

ensure that all philosophers pick up the left fork before the right fork

B.

ensure that all philosophers pick up the right fork before the left fork

C.

ensure that one particular philosopher picks up the left fork before the right fork, and that all other philosophers pick up the right fork before the left fork
questions.correct_answer:
C.

ensure that one particular philosopher picks up the left fork before the right fork, and that all other philosophers pick up the right fork before the left fork
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Which of the following is an example of spooled device?

(GATE CSE 1996)
options:
A.

A line printer used to print the output of a number of jobs

B.

A terminal used to enter input data to a running program

C.

A secondary storage device in a virtual memory system
questions.correct_answer:
A.

A line printer used to print the output of a number of jobs

questions.question:
